title: Dates
content:
### The Date Object

The **Date** object lets you work with dates and times in JavaScript.

---

### Creating Dates

```js
new Date()                    // Current date/time
new Date(milliseconds)        // Since Jan 1, 1970 (epoch)
new Date("2024-01-15")       // From a date string
new Date(2024, 0, 15)        // Year, Month (0-11), Day
new Date(2024, 0, 15, 10, 30, 0) // With time
```

> **Important:** Months are **0-indexed** (0 = January, 11 = December).

---

### Getting Date Components

| Method | Returns | Example |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `getFullYear()` | 4-digit year | `2024` |
| `getMonth()` | Month (0-11) | `0` (January) |
| `getDate()` | Day of month (1-31) | `15` |
| `getDay()` | Day of week (0-6) | `1` (Monday) |
| `getHours()` | Hours (0-23) | `10` |
| `getMinutes()` | Minutes (0-59) | `30` |
| `getSeconds()` | Seconds (0-59) | `0` |
| `getTime()` | Milliseconds since epoch | `1705312200000` |

---

### Setting Date Components

```js
const d = new Date();
d.setFullYear(2025);
d.setMonth(11);     // December
d.setDate(25);
d.setHours(12);
```

---

### Formatting Dates

```js
const d = new Date();

console.log(d.toString());       // "Mon Jan 15 2024 10:30:00..."
console.log(d.toDateString());   // "Mon Jan 15 2024"
console.log(d.toTimeString());   // "10:30:00 GMT+0530..."
console.log(d.toISOString());    // "2024-01-15T05:00:00.000Z"
console.log(d.toLocaleDateString()); // Locale format (e.g., "1/15/2024")
console.log(d.toLocaleTimeString()); // Locale time format
```

---

### Date Calculations

```js
const today = new Date();
const tomorrow = new Date(today);
tomorrow.setDate(today.getDate() + 1);

// Difference in milliseconds
const diff = tomorrow - today;  // 86400000 ms = 1 day

// Convert to days
const diffDays = diff / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24); // 1
```

> **Gotcha:** Dates are stored as milliseconds. Arithmetic works, but be careful with time zones and daylight saving.

quiz.question: What month does getMonth() return for January?
